description: Per-tier budget caps for Test Suite v2, enforced by scripts/testing-v2/assert-budget.mjs. WALL is the hard gate (read from runner JSON reports / the run-v2 wall clock). CPU-min is OBSERVABILITY only: the process-tree sampler sums per-PID cumulative CPU across the whole run and, on a shared/busy box, demonstrably over-counts past the physical ceiling (cores × wall) via PID churn and ppid misattribution — so maxCpuMs values are generous sanity ceilings (catch a gross regression), not tight gates. This supersedes the old '≤13 CPU-min, caps sum to full' policy per design.md §D7 (budget increase accepted in exchange for zero-flake reliability). Values marked provisional are confirmed by the committed concurrency-proof + a clean isolated run.

tier1.label: vitest core + dom + integration
tier1.note: ≤120 s wall isolated. Not gated by a script today (test:v2:core = plain vitest run); kept for reference + the tier-scoped assert-budget CLI.

tier2.label: playwright-v2 browser (geometry adapters + smoke journeys)
tier2.note: ≤3600 s wall. Enforced by test:v2:browser via assert-budget browser --report. crash/restart + heavy fixtures live in the daily lane.

full.label: npm run test:v2 (tier1 ∥ tier2, single isolated run)
full.note: ≤3600 s wall. maxCpuMs is an observability ceiling; CPU is not a tight gate (see top-level description).

concurrency.note: Under-load bar (this 24-core box): 3 concurrent test:v2 runs × 3 reps = 9/9 green, retries:0, ZERO flakes, each run ≤ perRunMaxWallMs (600 s / 10 min) under mutual load, ledger keeps sum(workerSlots) ≤ cores (24). Concurrency capped at 3 (not 5): the measured 5-way failure is a hardware/gateway-boot capacity limit — v2-integration + browser tests each boot a full gateway, so 5 concurrent full suites saturate 24 cores and starve heavy integration tests into timeouts (7080/7081 pass; one gate-reset-api timeout). Restoring 5-way is deferred to a spin-off goal (reduce per-test gateway cost / share a gateway). When BOBBIT_V2_CONCURRENCY_RUN=1, assert-budget switches EVERY scope's wall cap to perRunMaxWallMs and stops gating CPU (each concurrent run's process-tree sampler sees its siblings' descendants, so CPU-min is meaningless under load). perRunMaxCpuMs is recorded for observability only.
